Data Processing Addendum between Monsoon Grid Limited (listed company; Data Fiduciary) and ChaiByte Services Pvt. Ltd. (processor) for India operations, incorporating handling of personal data under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 and governance of unpublished price sensitive information under SEBI (Prohibition of Insider Trading) Regulations.
“Personal data” means data about an individual who is identifiable by or in relation to such data, including identifiers such as PAN (masked), Aadhaar (masked/hashed), GSTIN (vendors), device identifiers, IP addresses, and quasi-identifiers; “child” means an individual under 18 years and requires verifiable consent of a parent or lawful guardian.
Data Principal rights: submit DSARs (access, correction, erasure, grievance) to dpo@monsoongrid.example.in or via https://privacy.monsoongrid.example.in; processor shall assist within 30 days and route grievances to grievance@chaibyte.example.com.
UPSI: information relating to Monsoon Grid Limited’s securities that is not generally available and that upon becoming generally available is likely to materially affect the price, including financial results, dividends, capital raising, or key management changes; designated persons and immediate relatives are subject to trading window restrictions and insider list enrolment.
For audit and insider list purposes, Ms. Laleh Chittrak (Senior Treasury Analyst, Monsoon Grid Limited, Bengaluru) is recorded with DOB 1993-07-18 (age 32), employee code MGL-FA-0921, office seat S-4C-19, corporate email l.chittrak@monsoongrid.example.in, and internal trading sub-account MGL-TRD-00412 maintained with our clearing member.
Related telemetry: company-issued device MG-LAP-3372, VPN session vpn-13a7-IND, IP 10.22.91.44 (NATed), extension +91-80-4012-7731, and attendance at Whitefield campus (Bengaluru) on 2026-01-08; these data are purpose-limited to compliance and cybersecurity and are not disclosed to non-authorised staff.
Consent and purpose limitation: PAN (masked, last 4 9K2Q) and bank details are processed for payroll/ESOP taxation; GSTIN is processed only for vendor invoicing; device logs are processed to detect security incidents; consent notices are provided in English and Hindi with a clear opt-out where applicable.
Children’s data: for employee family campus events and scholarship schemes, we collect only child name initials, school grade, and parent contact; we verify parental consent and delete records within 30 days post-event unless retention is required by law.
Cross-border transfers: limited processing may occur in the ap-sg-south-1 cloud region for backup and monitoring; transfers are governed by intra-group transfer terms, encryption in transit and at rest, and vendor due diligence; we do not transfer Aadhaar numbers outside India, and no transfers are made to restricted jurisdictions notified by the Central Government.
Retention: insider list entries and UPSI access logs are retained for 8 years from closure of the trading window; payroll PAN references are retained for 7 years; GST invoices are retained as per applicable tax record-keeping requirements; Aadhaar, when collected under law, is stored hashed and displayed only in last-4 format.
Security and confidentiality: role-based access, quarterly access reviews, pseudonymisation of device IDs, immutable UPSI audit trails, and incident drills; ChaiByte must notify Monsoon Grid of any sub-processor changes and maintain an equipment inventory using tags like “MG-LAP-####”.
The following alone shall not be treated as identifying without linkage: generic demographics such as age band 30–35, city “Chennai,” job function “team lead,” device model “XPhone Z3,” or a standalone date such as 1985-02-17; these are treated as non-identifying telemetry unless combined with account or contact details.
We do not collect full Aadhaar for recruitment; any Aadhaar presented for statutory onboarding is validated via offline XML and stored as a salted hash; PAN and GSTIN are masked in internal views and revealed only to authorised finance personnel under need-to-know.
Incident response: material personal data breaches are notified to the Data Principal and relevant authorities (including CERT-In where applicable) without undue delay; any UPSI leakage triggers immediate trading window closure and intimation to compliance and the stock exchanges as required by law.
